risk_title: Institutional Capacity and Coordination Gaps Affecting Project Implementation
risk_summary: Technical Implementing Ministries and Agencies (TIMAs) are causing delays and bottlenecks in project implementation, with activities submitted for funding outside the Annual Work Plan. The Project Technical Committee lacks regular meetings and strategic coordination.
evidence_quote: The mission noted that implementation of the Project is challenging as technical implementing entities (MDA's) in most cases cause delays or bottlenecks to the implementation of project activities and submit those activities for funding outside of the Annual Work Plan.

risk_title: Governance Fragility in Project Coordination and Fiduciary Management
risk_summary: Significant delays in procurement, contract management, and payments to consultants and contractors are affecting project implementation. The Project Fiduciary Management Unit (PFMU) is not efficiently handling payments, creating implementation bottlenecks.
evidence_quote: The mission noted significant delays in procurement/evaluation processes, contract management, payment to consultants, contractors and grant disbursements. The Project Coordinator should accelerate the procurement process and ensure that the PFMU is efficient in handling payments.

risk_title: Misuse of Grant Funds and Accountability Issues Among Beneficiaries
risk_summary: Multiple SME grantees have misused project funds. While 5 out of 9 cases in Cohort 1 have been resolved, 3 remain unresolved and have been referred to the Ministry of Finance legal team. An additional case identified in Cohort 2 is still being addressed.
evidence_quote: These relate to identified case of the misuse of grant funds by some SMEs. The project however has successfully resolved 5 cases out the 9 SMEs in Cohort 1 which has been found wanting. An additional case has been identified amongst cohort 2 and the PCU continues to work with the service provider to resolve this case. Unresolved cases have been referred to the Ministry of Finance legal team for further action.

risk_title: Weak Occupational Health and Safety Compliance at Construction Sites
risk_summary: Occupational Health and Safety (OHS) gaps observed at all three destination sites (Bureh Beach, Tacugama Chimpanzee Sanctuary, Leicester Peak), including workers not wearing appropriate PPE despite visible hazards. Environmental and Social Officers not consistently present at sites.
evidence_quote: Some Occupational Health and Safety (OHS) gaps were observed at all three facilities, namely: Bureh Beach, Tacugama Chimpanzee Sanctuary and Leicester Peak

risk_title: Inadequate Sexual Exploitation and Abuse (SEA/SH) Service Provider Engagement
risk_summary: The project has not formalized a relationship with a GBV/SEA/SH service provider. Discussions with RAINBO Initiatives stalled due to cost disagreements. This is particularly concerning given predominantly young, female food sellers at construction sites with low awareness of SEA/SH risks.
evidence_quote: The project, has to date, not formalized a relationship with a GBV/SEA/SH service provider. The project had commenced discussion with RAINBO Initiatives – an NGO in Freetown which provides services to survivors of sexual exploitation, abuse, and sexual harassment. However, the discussion stalled due to disagreements on the cost of service.

risk_title: Loss of Key Environmental Specialist Capacity at PCU
risk_summary: The substantive Environmental Specialist at the Project Coordination Unit vacated their position in March 2024, creating a gap in environmental and social risk management oversight during critical construction phases.
evidence_quote: The Bank was informed that the substantive Environmental Specialist at the Project Coordination Unit (PCU) has vacated her position since March 2024.

risk_title: Unverified Sources of Construction Materials Raising Child Labor and Illegal Mining Concerns
risk_summary: The project has not verified the source of construction materials (sand and quarry materials) being used by contractors. Materials could potentially be sourced from illegal or unaccredited sellers, including those mining from coastal lines and habitats or utilizing child labor.
evidence_quote: The project, has to date, not verified the source of construction materials (sand and quarry materials) being used by contractors. This was a concern because the materials could potentially be sourced from illegal or unaccredited sellers including those mining from coastal lines and places of habitat or utilizing child labor.

risk_title: Community Land Acquisition Delays Affecting Project Infrastructure
risk_summary: The project has not been successful in acquiring land from the community at Bureh Beach for construction of mechanized borehole and toilet facilities. This delays critical infrastructure and risks community tensions over project benefits.
evidence_quote: The Mission was informed that the project has, to date, not been successful in acquiring land from the community for the construction of a mechanized borehole and a toilet facility for the community.
